items.title: Create a Separate List for 2027 New Grad Roles
items.description:
### Improvement

2027 new graduate roles have started opening, but they are currently mixed with roles intended for earlier graduating classes.

It would be helpful to create a separate section, page, or table specifically for **2027 New Grad positions**.

### Suggested changes

- Add a dedicated list for roles targeting students graduating in 2027.
- Clearly display the eligible graduation year or graduation window for each position.
- Keep 2026 and 2027 roles separated so applicants can quickly find relevant opportunities.
- Optionally add a graduation-year filter if maintaining separate lists becomes difficult.

This would make the repository easier to navigate and prevent students from applying to roles for which they are not eligible.

items.description:
### Link to Job Posting

https://www.projectvnext.com/software-engineer

### Company Name

Project vNext

### Job Title

Software Engineer

### Location

Remote (USA)

### What category does this job belong to?

Software Engineering

### Does this job offer sponsorship?

U.S. Citizenship is Required

### Is this job posting currently accepting applications?

Yes

### Advanced Degree Requirements

- [ ] This position requires a Master's degree or higher

### Email associated with your GitHub account (Optional)

derek.rushing@projectvnext.com

### Extra Notes (Optional)

Project vNext builds software for both internal products and external clients, with a primary focus on financial technology.

We're a small team that values ownership, pragmatism, discipline, and execution. We care more about an engineer's ability to learn, solve problems, and consistently deliver quality work than whether they've worked with a specific framework or technology before.

items.title: Albertson roles are NOT SWE positions.
items.description:
### Issue/Question

Take this for example: https://eofd.fa.us6.oraclecloud.com/hcmUI/CandidateExperience/en/sites/CX_1001/job/718586?utm_source=Simplify&ref=Simplify

This, in the README, is refereed to as the role: **Entry Level Front End Engineer**
If you read the description of the posting (which is just labeled **Front End Entry Level**), you realize that this is not a SWE role.

**Front End Entry Level**: Description
> When you begin your job with us working on the front-end as a courtesy clerk, customer service associate, or cashier, you are taking a step towards an opportunity that can help you learn, grow, and even develop a long-lasting career! We know you're choosing to invest your time with us, and we are committed to doing the same for you through on-the-job and online learning opportunities! Whether you are here for your first job or your last, from groceries to health needs, you guide, inspire, and make all customers' visit memorable. We build belonging by creating a diverse and inclusive workforce so, if you have smiles to share, we'd love to have you on our team.

This is true of all of the Albertson's roles I've looked at
